testimonials.title: Perfect blend of professional and fun
testimonials.content: We hired DJ Alex for our annual Lone Star Tech Solutions summit, and the experience exceeded expectations. The sound was flawless, the music fit our diverse crowd, and the short team-building dance break was a huge hit. Our team is still talking about it.

testimonials.title: Engaging DJ for our corporate holiday party
testimonials.content: The DJ struck the perfect balance between background music during dinner and a packed dance floor later in the night. He followed our guidelines and still kept things lively.

testimonials.title: The dance floor was full all night
testimonials.content: From the ceremony music to the last salsa track, everything was seamless. Our guests loved the mix of Latin and Top 40, and the lighting made the room look magical.

testimonials.title: Amazing bilingual MC and DJ
testimonials.content: Our families speak both English and Spanish, and he navigated both seamlessly as MC and DJ. The first dance choreography lessons were a huge plus.

testimonials.title: Best 30th birthday ever
testimonials.content: The DJ read the crowd perfectly and even mixed in some throwback tracks from my high school days. The lighting made my backyard look like a club.

testimonials.title: Sweet 16 with nonstop dancing
testimonials.content: He kept the music clean, fun, and current for my daughter's Sweet 16. The wireless mic made speeches easy, and setup was very professional.

testimonials.title: Patient and encouraging instructor
testimonials.content: I was nervous about taking my first salsa lesson, but he broke everything down clearly and made it fun. I left feeling confident and excited to keep learning.

testimonials.title: Great partner for our fundraiser
testimonials.content: He donated time for our community fundraiser, kept the vibe upbeat, and got people dancing between performances.

faq_items.question: How far do you travel for events?
faq_items.answer: Standard travel is included for events within 50 miles of central Austin, TX. For events beyond 50 miles, we charge an additional travel fee of $1.50 per mile to cover time, fuel, and vehicle wear. Depending on the distance and event end time, an overnight stay may also be required and will be discussed in your quote.

faq_items.question: Which cities and areas do you regularly serve?
faq_items.answer: Most of our events are in the greater Austin area, including Round Rock, Cedar Park, Pflugerville, Georgetown, Buda, and Kyle. We also frequently travel to San Antonio, San Marcos, Waco, Houston, and Dallas for weddings and corporate events, subject to additional travel fees.

faq_items.question: Do I need to provide parking or load-in details?
faq_items.answer: Yes. For all events, we ask that you share parking, loading dock, and elevator details in advance. If your venue charges for parking or requires long-distance load-ins, these costs should be covered by the client.

faq_items.question: How far in advance should we book you?
faq_items.answer: For peak wedding and corporate dates (spring and fall weekends), we recommend booking 9–12 months in advance. For private parties and smaller events, 2–4 months is usually enough. Last-minute dates may be available, so it never hurts to ask.

faq_items.question: Can you hold a date while we decide?
faq_items.answer: Dates are confirmed only after we receive a signed agreement and deposit. We can place a soft hold for up to 7 days while you review your proposal, but if another inquiry comes in for the same date, we will reach out to you before releasing the hold.

faq_items.question: Can we change our event details after booking?
faq_items.answer: Yes. You can update your timeline, song requests, and basic details up to 7 days before the event. Major changes such as a new venue, different date, or significantly altered hours may affect pricing and are subject to availability.

faq_items.question: Is a deposit required to secure our date?
faq_items.answer: Yes. A non-refundable retainer of 30% of the total package price is required to secure your date. The remaining balance is typically due 7 days before the event unless otherwise stated in your agreement.

faq_items.question: What forms of payment do you accept?
faq_items.answer: We accept credit and debit cards, ACH bank transfers, Zelle, and major digital wallets. Cash is accepted for in-person private lessons only. Checks are accepted for corporate and institutional clients with prior approval.

faq_items.question: What is your cancellation and refund policy?
faq_items.answer: The retainer paid to secure your date is non-refundable. If you cancel more than 60 days before the event, any additional payments made beyond the retainer are refundable. Cancellations within 60 days of the event are subject to our full fee, though we will always try to work with you on rescheduling when possible.

faq_items.question: How do private lessons work and how do I book?
faq_items.answer: You can request private lessons directly through the Private Lessons page by choosing a time from the calendar and submitting the request form. Once we confirm your request by email, you’ll receive a booking link and payment instructions. Lessons are available at the studio, online, or at your location (for an additional travel fee).

faq_items.question: What is your rescheduling policy for private lessons?
faq_items.answer: You can reschedule a private lesson up to 24 hours before the start time with no penalty. Changes made within 24 hours of the lesson may be charged as a full lesson, as it is difficult to fill the slot on short notice.

faq_items.question: What should I wear and bring to a dance lesson?
faq_items.answer: Wear comfortable clothing that allows you to move freely. For salsa and bachata, low- or medium-heeled shoes with smooth soles are ideal. Bring water, a small towel if you tend to sweat, and a list of any songs you love dancing to.
